title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'We want articles about the impact of social media on friendships. How has social media changed the way we make and keep friends? Has it been a positive or negative change?'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Social Media: Friend or Foe for Friendships?

Over the past decade, social media has completely transformed the way we connect with other people. But has this change been for better or worse?

On the positive side, platforms like Instagram and WhatsApp make it incredibly easy to stay in touch with old friends, even when they live in different countries. Before social media, losing contact with a school friend was almost inevitable. Now, a quick message keeps those relationships alive.

However, there is a downside. Many people argue that online friendships lack depth. Instead of having meaningful conversations, we simply scroll through each other's highlights and press 'like'. This can create a false sense of connection without real emotional support.

In my opinion, social media is a useful tool, but it cannot replace face-to-face interaction. The key is to use it wisely — to strengthen real friendships rather than substitute them. Quality always matters more than the number of followers.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
You recently joined an online language learning community and made a new friend called Alex. Alex has asked you to write an email explaining why you joined the community, what you have learned so far, and whether you would recommend it to others.

Write your email to Alex.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Alex,

Thank you so much for getting in touch! I'm really glad we connected in the community — it makes the whole experience much more enjoyable.

I originally joined the online language learning community because I wanted to improve my English before a job interview. I had been studying on my own, but I felt I needed more practice with real people. A friend recommended the platform, and it turned out to be exactly what I needed.

So far, I've improved my writing skills enormously and I've also become much more confident when speaking. The weekly live sessions with native speakers have been particularly useful for building fluency.

Would I recommend it? Absolutely! I think anyone who is serious about learning a language should join. The community is friendly, the resources are excellent, and the progress you make is very noticeable. Just make sure you commit to practising every day — consistency is the secret!

Hope to chat again soon,
Sam

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'Have you used a social media app or platform that you think is particularly good or bad for young people? Write a review telling us about it. Describe its features and say whether you would recommend it.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
App Review: Duolingo — More Than Just Language Learning

Duolingo is a free language-learning app that has grown into one of the most popular educational tools in the world. But does it really live up to the hype?

The app's greatest strength is its design. Lessons are short, colourful, and game-like, which makes it feel more like playing than studying. Each session lasts only about five minutes, which is ideal for busy students. The streak system encourages you to practise daily, creating a healthy habit without too much pressure.

In terms of content, Duolingo covers a wide range of skills including reading, listening, and speaking. However, the grammar explanations can be limited, so it works best when combined with other resources.

For young people especially, Duolingo is a fantastic starting point. It builds confidence, makes language learning accessible, and most importantly, it keeps you motivated. Occasional notifications can feel annoying, but this is a minor issue.

Overall, I would strongly recommend Duolingo to anyone looking to start or maintain a new language. It is fun, free, and genuinely effective.
